entities.name: RED SOILS
entities.description: Soils characterized by red color, analyzed for physical and chemical properties in India's semi-arid tropics

entities.name: BLACK SOILS
entities.description: Soils characterized by black color, often Vertisols, analyzed for physical and chemical properties in India's semi-arid tropics

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide in the soil to mitigate climate change

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region characterized by low and irregular rainfall, specifically in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the benchmark spots for soil carbon sequestration studies are located

entities.name: SAND
entities.description: A coarse soil particle size used to describe the physical properties of red and black soils

entities.name: SILT
entities.description: A medium soil particle size used to describe the physical properties of red and black soils

entities.name: CLAY
entities.description: The smallest soil particle size, significantly affecting water retention and nutrient capacity

entities.name: FINE CLAY
entities.description: Clay particles smaller than 0.2 micrometers, showing a positive correlation with organic carbon

entities.name: BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of dry soil per unit volume, showing negative correlation with organic carbon

entities.name: COLE
entities.description: A physical property of soil related to expansion and contraction, positively correlated with total clay

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ease with which water can move through soil pores, showing a decreasing trend with organic carbon

entities.name: WATER DROPLET CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: A measure of water movement in soil used as a physical characteristic

entities.name: PH
entities.description: The measure of acidity or alkalinity of the soil, measured in H2O and KCl

entities.name: ELECTRICAL CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: A measure of the salt concentration in the soil

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: Carbon stored in soil organic matter, varying by land use system and bioclimatic condition

entities.name: CALCIUM CARBONATE
entities.description: Inorganic carbon found in soil as CaCO3, showing an increasing trend with exchangeable sodium percentage

entities.name: EXTRACTABLE CALCIUM
entities.description: The amount of calcium available for plant uptake in the soil

entities.name: MAGNESIUM
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient found in the chemical analysis of the soils

entities.name: SODIUM
entities.description: An element found in soil, often associated with high exchangeable sodium percentage

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient found in the chemical analysis of the soils

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: The total capacity of a soil to hold exchangeable cations

entities.name: BASE SATURATION
entities.description: The proportion of the cation exchange sites occupied by basic cations

entities.name: EXCHANGEABLE SODIUM PERCENTAGE
entities.description: The percentage of the cation exchange capacity occupied by sodium, affecting SOC and SIC levels

entities.name: SUB-HUMID
entities.description: A bioclimatic system with moderate moisture, categorized as moist or dry

entities.name: SEMI-ARID
entities.description: A bioclimatic system with low moisture, categorized as moist or dry

entities.name: ARID
entities.description: A bioclimatic system with very low moisture

entities.name: SLICKENSIDES
entities.description: Smooth, polished surfaces in clay-rich soils caused by swelling and shrinking, correlated with COLE

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: Clay-rich soils that shrink and swell, common in the semi-arid tropics of India

entities.name: FOREST SYSTEM
entities.description: A land use system dominated by trees, showing the highest surface organic carbon values

entities.name: PERMANENT FALLOW
entities.description: Land left uncultivated for a long period, often as grassland

entities.name: HORTICULTURAL SYSTEM
entities.description: A land use system for fruits, vegetables, and ornamental plants

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL SYSTEM
entities.description: A land use system for crop production, where SOC varies by crop type

entities.name: WASTELAND
entities.description: Land that is not suitable for cultivation, showing low organic carbon values

entities.name: SUN HEMP
entities.description: A legume used for green manuring in crop rotation to increase soil organic carbon

entities.name: CEREAL
entities.description: Grass-based crops grown for their edible seeds, showing higher SOC but lower SIC

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: A legume crop grown for oil and protein, showing moderate SOC and SIC

entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: A fiber crop, showing lower SOC but the highest SIC values

entities.name: GREEN MANURING
entities.description: The practice of plowing under a green crop like sun hemp to improve soil fertility

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing a series of different types of crops in the same area across a sequence of seasons
